feedback.text: I'd like to ask that the Digital Fairness Act include clear protections for people who buy digital games and software. Right now, its unfortunately common for games to be shut down entirely, even if they were mostly single-player. A well-known example is The Crew, which people paid full price for and can no longer access at all. That kind of practice is extremely unfair to consumers, and I believe it should be addressed in EU legislation. If a company sells a game, there should be a requirement that it remains playable in some form. Either with an offline mode, or by allowing the community to run their own servers after official ones are shut down. Otherwise, its not a real purchase, just a temporary rental disguised as ownership. Millions of Europeans have already signed the Stop Killing Games/Stop Destroying Games petition, showing how important this issue is to players across the EU. I hope the Digital Fairness Act takes this seriously and helps make digital ownership more fair and reliable for everyone. Thank you.
